intro.title: Welcome to R
intro.bodyMarkdown:
R is a language and environment for statistical computing and graphics, widely used in academia, research, and industry.

Practicing this topic builds fluency with R syntax, data structures, and the tidyverse ecosystem.

Run the sample code below, experiment in the editor, then work through the challenges.

challenges.title: Easy: Print hello R
challenges.promptMarkdown: Print exactly `Hello, R!`

challenges.title: Easy: Print a number
challenges.promptMarkdown: Print `42` on its own line.

challenges.title: Easy: Two print lines
challenges.promptMarkdown: Print `Line 1` then `Line 2` on separate lines.

challenges.title: Medium: Math in print
challenges.promptMarkdown: Print the result of `6 * 7`.

challenges.title: Medium: Combine text
challenges.promptMarkdown: Print `I am learning R.` exactly.

challenges.title: Medium: Variable print
challenges.promptMarkdown: Set `name <- "Ada"` and print `name`.

challenges.title: Hard: Paste strings
challenges.promptMarkdown: Print `Hello World` using `paste("Hello", "World")`.

challenges.title: Expert: Three-line program
challenges.promptMarkdown: Print three lines: your language name, `2024`, and `Ready to analyze!`

challenges.title: Expert: Version info
challenges.promptMarkdown: Print `R` using `print("R")`.

questions.prompt: R is primarily designed for:
Which function prints output in R?
R is widely used for:
What assignment operator is preferred in R?
Missing values in R are represented by:
Which function combines values into a vector?
Data frames are:
Type the function to get help on a topic.
Expert: Vectorized operations in R:
Expert: Name one keyword from What is R.

levels.title: R vs Python and Other Languages

intro.title: R vs Python and Other Languages
intro.bodyMarkdown:
R vs Python and Other Languages is a core topic when learning R for data analysis, statistics, and reproducible research.

Practicing this topic builds fluency with R syntax, data structures, and the tidyverse ecosystem.

Run the sample code below, experiment in the editor, then work through the challenges.
intro.sandboxCode:
# R excels at statistics; Python excels at general software
print("Compare tools to the task")

challenges.title: Easy: Use print
challenges.promptMarkdown: Write R code that uses `print` related to **R vs Python and Other Languages**.

challenges.title: Easy: Print a number
challenges.promptMarkdown: Print the number `100` using `print()`.

challenges.title: Easy: Assign a variable
challenges.promptMarkdown: Create variable `x` with `<-` and set it to `5`, then print `x`.

challenges.title: Medium: Apply R vs Python and Other Languages
challenges.promptMarkdown: Write code demonstrating **R vs Python and Other Languages**.

challenges.title: Medium: Two-step snippet
challenges.promptMarkdown: Use `print` and `print` in one script.

challenges.title: Medium: Vector basics
challenges.promptMarkdown: Create `v <- c(1, 2, 3)` and print `sum(v)`.

challenges.title: Hard: Conditional output
challenges.promptMarkdown: Set `n <- 10`. If `n > 0`, print `"positive"`.

challenges.title: Hard: Function call
challenges.promptMarkdown: Use `length()` on `c(4, 8, 15)` and print the result.

challenges.title: Expert: Topic practice
challenges.promptMarkdown: Combine ideas from **R vs Python and Other Languages** in a short script using `print`.

challenges.title: Expert: Multi-line script
challenges.promptMarkdown: Write at least two lines of R related to **R vs Python and Other Languages** (include `print`).
challenges.starterCode: # R excels at statistics; Python excels at general software

questions.prompt: R is often compared to Python because both are used for:
Which function prints output in R?
R is widely used for:
What assignment operator is preferred in R?
Missing values in R are represented by:
Which function combines values into a vector?
Data frames are:
Type the function to get help on a topic.
Expert: Vectorized operations in R:
Expert: Name one keyword from R vs Python and Other Languages.

intro.title: Installing R

Installing R is a core topic when learning R for data analysis, statistics, and reproducible research.

Practicing this topic builds fluency with R syntax, data structures, and the tidyverse ecosystem.

Run the sample code below, experiment in the editor, then work through the challenges.

# After install, verify in the console:
